label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12

extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAS Richard attacked from 32-rider break on final climb to take stage, while POGACAR survived crash and GC attacks to retain red jersey unchanged
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma attacked repeatedly on final climb despite no time gaps materializing, revealing ineffective pressure tactics against resilient Pogacar.
32-rider break allowed with 4 teams unrepresented indicates peloton miscalculation on selective final climb's GC potential.
Carapas as team principal rather than pure GC rider enabled aggressive freedom from breakaway that domestique role previously constrained.
Kelderman moved to 6th GC via breakaway positioning with Fabro pacing, showing Bora using stage hunting for GC advancement.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: absorbed crash and sustained GC attacks on final climb without losing time, then counterattacked at finish
rider_signals.CARAPAS Richard: positive: won from large break after role change to team principal freed him from domestique duties
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: struggled on final climb after two prior stage wins, suggesting accumulated fatigue affecting climbing form
rider_signals.POLANC Jan: positive: survived difficult final climb despite struggling early to protect top-10 GC position
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: moved into top-6 GC using breakaway rather than peloton tactics, with strong Fabro support
rider_signals.FABRO Mattia: positive: paced Kelderman through entire final climb from breakaway, demonstrating domestique excellence in reduced group
